modules.module_title: Introduction to Yoga, Philosophy, History & Patanjali's Ashtanga Yoga
Study of Asanas: Standing, Sitting, Prone & Supine Postures
Human Anatomy, Physiology of Yoga, Stress Management & Meditation
Safety, Alignment, Sequencing, Props & Adapting for Special Needs
Professional Ethics, Class Management & Building Yoga Studio Brand

questions.question: Yoga Teacher – Foundation Level program ka primary goal kya hota hai?

options.A: Students ko Asanas, Pranayama aur Yoga philosophy ke correct principles seekhana aur safely teach karna
options.B: Developing medical drugs in laboratory setups
options.C: Building high rise weight training machinery
options.D: Zero spiritual or body alignment check

questions.explanation: Yoga teacher program asanas, breath works (pranayama), body anatomy, teaching alignment aur ethical studio structures ko design karta hai.

questions.question: Patanjali ke 'Ashtanga Yoga' (Eight Limbs) me first limb 'Yama' kya represent karta hai?

options.A: Social ethics aur moral restraints (eg Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya, Brahmacharya, Aparigraha)

questions.explanation: Yama life ethics aur external behavior restraint guidelines hai, which forms foundation of yoga path.

questions.question: Standing asanas checks me 'Tadasana' (Mountain Pose) ka primary function kya hai?

options.A: Improving body posture, balance, and establishing correct foot weight distribution alignments
options.C: Increasing heart rate to anaerobic limits fast

questions.explanation: Tadasana all standing asanas ka base posture hai. Ye body awareness, spine stretching aur ground balance improve karta hai.

questions.question: Pranayama techniques checks me 'Nadi Shodhana' (Alternate Nostril Breathing) ka main benefit?

options.A: Calming the nervous system, balancing left/right brain hemispheres and lowering stress levels
options.B: Raising blood pressure rapidly to boost sprint speeds

questions.explanation: Nadi Shodhana breathing loops left (Ida) aur right (Pingala) nadis energy flow ko balance karti hai, relieving mental pressure.

questions.question: Yoga physiology me human energy locks ko kya kaha jata hai?

options.A: Bandhas (eg Mula Bandha, Uddiyana Bandha, Jalandhara Bandha)
options.D: Locks do not exist in yoga

questions.explanation: Bandhas energy locks hain jo internal prana flow channels validate karte hain during breath retention (Kumbhaka).

questions.question: Sitting asanas checks me 'Paschimottanasana' (Seated Forward Bend) me primary stretch zone?

options.A: Hamstrings and entire posterior spine muscles

questions.explanation: Forward bending hamstrings spine muscles compress stretch improve karta hai, improving pelvic flexibility.

questions.question: Yoga teaching logs checks me 'Props' (blocks, straps, bolsters) ka primary use case?

options.A: Supporting students with tight muscles to execute asanas safely without straining alignments
options.C: Using them as heavy lifting weight items
options.D: Props are strictly forbidden in traditional yoga

questions.explanation: Yoga props structure support karte hain for beginners or restricted clients, protecting lumbar region from overload.

questions.question: Yoga safety checks me, in case of high blood pressure patient, which asana group is restricted?

options.A: Deep inversions (eg Headstand - Sirsasana, Shoulderstand - Sarvangasana)

questions.explanation: Inversions head arteries par blood pressure excess build karte hain, which can trigger vessel damage in hypertension cases.

questions.question: Shatkarmas (Kriyas) checks me 'Jala Neti' cleaning process kis area ko target karta hai?

options.A: Nasal passages (using lukewarm saline water and neti pot)

questions.explanation: Jala Neti nasal cleaning and sinus disinfection checklist target karta hai, clearing respiratory pathways.

questions.question: Yoga class ethics checks me teacher alignment touch guidelines?

options.A: Always ask verbal permission before performing physical hands-on adjustments on students
options.B: Perform adjustments forcefully without asking any notifications
options.D: Ethics codes do not apply to adjustment touches

questions.explanation: Asking consent respects participant boundary comfort levels, keeping the classroom safe and professional.

questions.question: Patanjali's Yoga Sutra defines Yoga as 'Yoga Chitta Vritti Nirodha'. What does this translate to?

options.A: Yoga is the calming of the fluctuations of the mind
options.B: Yoga is the performance of fast physical jumps
options.C: Yoga is the control of financial thoughts

questions.explanation: Patanjali defines yoga as mastering mind fluctuations (vrittis) to reach internal silence (Samadhi).

questions.question: Patanjali's Ashtanga Yoga 'Niyama' (second limb) represents what aspect?

options.A: Personal disciplines and inner observances (eg Shaucha, Santosha, Tapas, Svadhyaya, Ishvara Pranidhana)

questions.explanation: Niyamas are personal practices (eg hygiene, contentness, study) that build inner self-discipline.

questions.question: Backbending asanas 'Bhujangasana' (Cobra Pose) belongs to which group?

options.A: Prone postures (lying flat on stomach facing floor)

questions.explanation: Bhujangasana is done in prone position. It strengthens the spine and opens the chest muscles.

questions.question: Pranayama terms 'Kumbhaka' signifies what breath state?

options.A: Breath retention (holding the breath either inside or outside)

questions.explanation: Kumbhaka is breath holding. Antar Kumbhaka is holding inside, and Bahir Kumbhaka is holding outside.

questions.question: Yoga anatomy checks 'Prana' flow path 'Sushumna Nadi' is located where?

options.A: Along the central spinal column (primary energy channel)

questions.explanation: Sushumna Nadi is the central energy channel along the spine. Kundalini energy rises through it during meditation.

options.D: Props are strictly forbidden in traditional yoga

options.A: Nasal passages (using lukewarm saline water and neti pot)

questions.explanation: Jala Neti nasal cleaning and sinus disinfection checklist target karta hai, clearing respiratory pathways.

options.B: Perform adjustments forcefully without asking any notifications
options.D: Ethics codes do not apply to adjustment touches

options.A: Yoga is the calming of the fluctuations of the mind
options.B: Yoga is the performance of fast physical jumps
options.C: Yoga is the control of financial thoughts

questions.explanation: Patanjali defines yoga as mastering mind fluctuations (vrittis) to reach internal silence (Samadhi).

questions.question: Ashtanga Niyama personal practices observances?

options.A: Personal disciplines and inner observances (eg Shaucha, Santosha, Tapas, Svadhyaya, Ishvara Pranidhana)

questions.explanation: Niyamas are personal practices (eg hygiene, contentness, study) that build inner self-discipline.

questions.question: Bhujangasana prone stomach flat?

options.A: Prone postures (lying flat on stomach facing floor)

questions.explanation: Bhujangasana is done in prone position. It strengthens the spine and opens the chest muscles.

questions.question: Kumbhaka breath retention inside outside?

options.A: Breath retention (holding the breath either inside or outside)

questions.explanation: Kumbhaka is breath holding. Antar Kumbhaka is holding inside, and Bahir Kumbhaka is holding outside.

questions.question: Sushumna Nadi central spinal spinal column?

options.A: Along the central spinal column (primary energy channel)

questions.explanation: Sushumna Nadi is the central energy channel along the spine. Kundalini energy rises through it during meditation.

questions.question: Pranayama techniques 'Kapalabhati' what is its breathing pattern?

options.A: Rapid, forceful exhalations with passive inhalations (skull-shining breath)
options.B: Slow deep breathing with voice sound (Ujjayi)

questions.explanation: Kapalabhati uses active abdominal exhalations and passive inhalations, clearing toxins from the respiratory system.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'The Three Gunas' what does Sattva represent?

options.A: Purity, light, harmony, and mental clarity
options.D: Gunas do not relate to mental states

questions.explanation: Sattva Guna brings balance and mental clarity, which is the goal of yoga practices.

questions.question: Asana classification 'Savasana' (Corpse Pose) why is it crucial at class endings?

options.A: Allows nervous system to integrate benefits of practice, lowering heart rate and initiating deep relaxation
options.C: Acts as a warm-up for running
options.D: Savasana can be skipped to save time

questions.explanation: Savasana calms the sympathetic nervous system, integrating the physical benefits of the class.

questions.question: Yoga anatomy 'Spine movements' what action describes backbending?

options.A: Spinal extension (moving spinal vertebrae backward)

questions.explanation: Backbends (like Bhujangasana) extend the spine, stretching the chest and abdomen muscles.

questions.question: Yoga teaching safety 'Pregnant client' which adjustments are recommended?

options.A: Avoid prone postures (flat on belly) and deep abdominal twists; use blocks/props for balance support
options.B: Encourage deep abdominal crunches to build strength
options.D: Pregnancy does not require workout modifications

questions.explanation: Avoiding prone poses and deep twists protects the fetus, keeping yoga practices safe during pregnancy.

questions.question: Kapalabhati rapid exhalation passive inhalation?

options.A: Rapid, forceful exhalations with passive inhalations (skull-shining breath)
options.B: Slow deep breathing with voice sound (Ujjayi)

questions.explanation: Kapalabhati uses active abdominal exhalations and passive inhalations, clearing toxins from the respiratory system.

questions.question: Sattva Guna purity harmony clarity?

options.A: Purity, light, harmony, and mental clarity
options.D: Gunas do not relate to mental states

questions.explanation: Sattva Guna brings balance and mental clarity, which is the goal of yoga practices.

options.D: Savasana can be skipped to save time

questions.explanation: Savasana calms the sympathetic nervous system, integrating the physical benefits of the class.

questions.question: Spinal extension backbending movement?

options.A: Spinal extension (moving spinal vertebrae backward)

questions.explanation: Backbends (like Bhujangasana) extend the spine, stretching the chest and abdomen muscles.

questions.question: Pregnancy yoga modifications avoid prone?

options.A: Avoid prone postures (flat on belly) and deep abdominal twists; use blocks/props for balance support
options.B: Encourage deep abdominal crunches to build strength
options.D: Pregnancy does not require workout modifications

questions.explanation: Avoiding prone poses and deep twists protects the fetus, keeping yoga practices safe during pregnancy.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'Patanjali Eight Limbs' what is Dharana?

options.A: Concentration (focusing the mind on a single point or object)

questions.explanation: Dharana is focusing the mind on a single point (eg candle flame), preparing it for deep meditation.

questions.question: Pranayama techniques 'Ujjayi' what sound is generated?

options.A: A soft whispering sound in throat (ocean breath sound generated by slightly constricting glottis)
options.B: A buzzing sound like a bee (Bhramari)

questions.explanation: Ujjayi uses glottis constriction to warm the breath, calming the nervous system during practices.

questions.question: Asana terminology 'Kona' what does it mean in asana names?

options.A: Angle (eg Trikonasana = Triangle Pose, Baddha Konasana = Bound Angle Pose)

questions.explanation: 'Kona' means angle. It forms the base of many pose names (eg Trikonasana translates to Three Angle Pose).

questions.question: Yoga anatomy 'Joint stability' what is primary stabilizer of knee joint?

options.A: Quadriceps muscles and ligaments (ACL, MCL, LCL)

questions.explanation: Strong quadriceps and ligaments stabilize the knee joint during load-bearing asanas.

questions.question: Yoga class sequencing 'The Peak Pose sequence' how is it structured?

options.A: Warm-up, preparatory poses, peak pose (hardest asana), counter poses, and final relaxation
options.B: Do peak pose first, then warm-up routines
options.C: Routines are kept completely random without sequencing
options.D: Sequencing does not affect muscle safety

questions.explanation: Peak pose sequencing warms up target muscles first, lowering injury risk for advanced poses.

questions.question: Dharana concentration single point focus?

options.A: Concentration (focusing the mind on a single point or object)

questions.explanation: Dharana is focusing the mind on a single point (eg candle flame), preparing it for deep meditation.

options.A: A soft whispering sound in throat (ocean breath sound generated by slightly constricting glottis)
options.B: A buzzing sound like a bee (Bhramari)

questions.explanation: Ujjayi uses glottis constriction to warm the breath, calming the nervous system during practices.

questions.question: Kona angle Trikonasana meaning?

options.A: Angle (eg Trikonasana = Triangle Pose, Baddha Konasana = Bound Angle Pose)

questions.explanation: 'Kona' means angle. It forms the base of many pose names (eg Trikonasana translates to Three Angle Pose).

questions.question: Knee joint stabilizers quadriceps ligaments?

options.A: Quadriceps muscles and ligaments (ACL, MCL, LCL)

questions.explanation: Strong quadriceps and ligaments stabilize the knee joint during load-bearing asanas.

options.A: Warm-up, preparatory poses, peak pose (hardest asana), counter poses, and final relaxation
options.B: Do peak pose first, then warm-up routines
options.C: Routines are kept completely random without sequencing
options.D: Sequencing does not affect muscle safety

questions.explanation: Peak pose sequencing warms up target muscles first, lowering injury risk for advanced poses.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'Patanjali Eight Limbs' what is Pratyahara?

options.A: Withdrawal of senses from external objects to turn mind inward

questions.explanation: Pratyahara disconnects the senses from distractions, clearing the path for concentration and meditation.

questions.question: Pranayama techniques 'Bhramari' what is the mechanism?

options.A: Buzzing bee sound produced during slow exhalation (calms brain and reduces anger)

questions.explanation: Bhramari uses a soft humming sound to soothe the nervous system, helping ease anxiety.

questions.question: Shatkarmas 'Trataka' what is the practice?

options.A: Gazing steadily at a small point (like a candle flame) without blinking to improve concentration and eye health
options.B: Nasal cavity cleaning with warm oil

questions.explanation: Trataka trains focus by gazing at a candle flame, calming the mind and strengthening eye muscles.

questions.question: Yoga anatomy 'Vertebral column sections' how many vertebrae form the cervical spine?

options.A: 7 vertebrae (neck section)
options.D: Spine is a single bone piece

questions.explanation: The cervical spine has 7 vertebrae (C1-C7), supporting neck rotation and flexibility.

questions.question: Yoga class sequencing 'Counter pose principle' what is counter pose for Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose)?

options.A: Balasana (Child's Pose) or Paschimottanasana (forward bending)
options.D: Bhujangasana does not require counter poses

questions.explanation: A forward bend (like Child's Pose) acts as a counter pose to backbends, stretching and relaxing the lower back.

questions.question: Pratyahara sense withdrawal inward?

options.A: Withdrawal of senses from external objects to turn mind inward

questions.explanation: Pratyahara disconnects the senses from distractions, clearing the path for concentration and meditation.

questions.question: Bhramari humming bee sound exhalation?

options.A: Buzzing bee sound produced during slow exhalation (calms brain and reduces anger)

questions.explanation: Bhramari uses a soft humming sound to soothe the nervous system, helping ease anxiety.

questions.question: Trataka candle gazing focus eye?

options.A: Gazing steadily at a small point (like a candle flame) without blinking to improve concentration and eye health
options.B: Nasal cavity cleaning with warm oil

questions.explanation: Trataka trains focus by gazing at a candle flame, calming the mind and strengthening eye muscles.

questions.question: Cervical spine 7 vertebrae neck?

options.A: 7 vertebrae (neck section)
options.D: Spine is a single bone piece

questions.explanation: The cervical spine has 7 vertebrae (C1-C7), supporting neck rotation and flexibility.

questions.question: Bhujangasana counter pose Balasana?

options.A: Balasana (Child's Pose) or Paschimottanasana (forward bending)
options.D: Bhujangasana does not require counter poses

questions.explanation: A forward bend (like Child's Pose) acts as a counter pose to backbends, stretching and relaxing the lower back.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'Patanjali Eight Limbs' what is Dhyana?

options.A: Unbroken flow of meditation (mind is fully absorbed in object of focus)

questions.explanation: Dhyana is continuous focus, where meditation flows naturally without effort.

questions.question: Pranayama techniques 'Shitali' what is the target effect on body?

options.A: Cooling effect (inhaling through rolled tongue lowers body temperature and thirst)
options.D: Shitali has zero effect on body heat

questions.explanation: Inhaling through a rolled tongue cools the breath, helping lower body temperature in hot weather.

questions.question: Yoga physiology 'Nadis total count' how many nadis (energy channels) are defined in traditional texts?

options.A: 72,000 nadis
options.D: Nadis are not counted in texts

questions.explanation: Traditional hatha texts list 72,000 nadis, with Ida, Pingala, and Sushumna being the primary channels.

questions.question: Yoga anatomy 'Hamstrings anatomy' how do tight hamstrings affect pelvic alignment in forward bends?

options.A: Pull pelvis backward (posterior tilt), causing rounding of the lower back (lumbar spine)
options.B: Allow pelvis to tilt forward easily
options.C: Tight hamstrings do not affect spinal curves

questions.explanation: Tight hamstrings limit pelvic tilt, placing stress on the lower back during forward folds.

questions.question: Yoga class safety 'Sciatica pain' which movement should be avoided?

options.A: Deep forward bending with straight knees (stretches sciatic nerve aggressively)
options.D: Sciatica does not require exercise modifications

questions.explanation: Deep forward folds put tension on the sciatic nerve. Keep knees slightly bent to prevent strain.

questions.question: Dhyana meditation absorption flow?

options.A: Unbroken flow of meditation (mind is fully absorbed in object of focus)

questions.explanation: Dhyana is continuous focus, where meditation flows naturally without effort.

questions.question: Shitali cooling rolled tongue breathing?

options.A: Cooling effect (inhaling through rolled tongue lowers body temperature and thirst)
options.D: Shitali has zero effect on body heat

questions.explanation: Inhaling through a rolled tongue cools the breath, helping lower body temperature in hot weather.

questions.question: Nadis 72000 energy channels?

options.A: 72,000 nadis
options.D: Nadis are not counted in texts

questions.explanation: Traditional hatha texts list 72,000 nadis, with Ida, Pingala, and Sushumna being the primary channels.

questions.question: Tight hamstrings posterior pelvic tilt?

options.A: Pull pelvis backward (posterior tilt), causing rounding of the lower back (lumbar spine)
options.B: Allow pelvis to tilt forward easily
options.C: Tight hamstrings do not affect spinal curves

questions.explanation: Tight hamstrings limit pelvic tilt, placing stress on the lower back during forward folds.

questions.question: Sciatica forward bending straight knees avoid?

options.A: Deep forward bending with straight knees (stretches sciatic nerve aggressively)
options.D: Sciatica does not require exercise modifications

questions.explanation: Deep forward folds put tension on the sciatic nerve. Keep knees slightly bent to prevent strain.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'Patanjali Ashtanga Yoga' what is Samadhi?

options.A: State of absolute absorption and union with the object of meditation (final limb)

questions.explanation: Samadhi is the final limb of yoga, representing complete absorption and inner peace.

questions.question: Pranayama physiology 'Ujjayi breathing' how does it affect heart rate?

options.A: Stimulates the vagus nerve, activating parasympathetic system to lower heart rate and blood pressure
options.B: Elevates heart rate to aerobic training zones
options.C: Restricts blood circulation to brain tissues

questions.explanation: Ujjayi stimulates the vagus nerve, calming the heart rate and blood pressure.

questions.question: Asana alignment 'Sirsasana' (Headstand) where should weight be distributed?

options.A: 80% of weight on forearms, with only minimal pressure on head (neck remains elongated)
options.B: 100% of weight directly on head and neck joints
options.C: Balanced on hands only with forearms raised
options.D: Weight distribution does not affect headstands

questions.explanation: Distributing weight to the forearms protects the cervical spine from strain during headstands.

questions.question: Yoga anatomy 'Planes of pelvic movement' what is anterior pelvic tilt?

options.A: Pelvis tilts forward, increasing arch in lower back (lumbar lordosis)
options.B: Pelvis tilts backward, flattening lower back arch

questions.explanation: Anterior tilt arches the lower back, which is common in backbending poses like cow pose.

questions.question: Yoga teacher ethics 'Scope of Practice' what is boundary limit when client seeks medical advice?

options.A: Politely decline medical diagnoses and refer client to licensed healthcare professionals
options.B: Prescribe ayurvedic medications and diet supplements yourself
options.C: Diagnose joint injuries using yoga tests

questions.explanation: Diagnosing injuries exceeds a yoga teacher's scope. Refer clients to medical professionals for clinical care.

questions.question: Samadhi absolute absorption union final limb?

options.A: State of absolute absorption and union with the object of meditation (final limb)

questions.explanation: Samadhi is the final limb of yoga, representing complete absorption and inner peace.

options.A: Stimulates the vagus nerve, activating parasympathetic system to lower heart rate and blood pressure
options.B: Elevates heart rate to aerobic training zones
options.C: Restricts blood circulation to brain tissues

questions.explanation: Ujjayi stimulates the vagus nerve, calming the heart rate and blood pressure.

questions.question: Sirsasana weight 80 forearms neck?

options.A: 80% of weight on forearms, with only minimal pressure on head (neck remains elongated)
options.B: 100% of weight directly on head and neck joints
options.C: Balanced on hands only with forearms raised
options.D: Weight distribution does not affect headstands

questions.explanation: Distributing weight to the forearms protects the cervical spine from strain during headstands.

questions.question: Anterior pelvic tilt arch lordosis?

options.A: Pelvis tilts forward, increasing arch in lower back (lumbar lordosis)
options.B: Pelvis tilts backward, flattening lower back arch

questions.explanation: Anterior tilt arches the lower back, which is common in backbending poses like cow pose.

options.B: Prescribe ayurvedic medications and diet supplements yourself
options.C: Diagnose joint injuries using yoga tests

questions.explanation: Diagnosing injuries exceeds a yoga teacher's scope. Refer clients to medical professionals for clinical care.

questions.question: Yoga philosophy 'The Five Koshas' (Sheaths of Being) what is Annamaya Kosha?

options.A: The physical body sheath (made of food and structural tissues)
options.D: Koshas do not relate to physical bodies

questions.explanation: Annamaya Kosha is the outer physical sheath, nourished by food and maintained through asana practice.

questions.question: Yoga teacher ethics 'Class safe spaces' what is guideline regarding students' physical limitations?

options.A: Respect individual limitations; modify poses and avoid forcing clients into painful alignments
options.B: Push clients past pain limits to speed up flexibility gains
options.C: Ignore limitations and teach one advanced sequence to all

questions.explanation: Respecting physical limits prevents injuries and builds a supportive, safe class environment.

questions.question: Yoga physiology 'The Parasympathetic Nervous System' which pranayama activates it fastest?

options.A: Chandra Bhedana (left nostril breathing) or Bhramari (humming bee breath)
options.D: Pranayama does not affect nervous systems

questions.explanation: Chandra Bhedana (left nostril) cools the body and activates the parasympathetic response, lowering heart rate.

questions.question: Asana alignment 'Trikonasana' (Triangle Pose) what is correct pelvic alignment?

options.A: Hips open laterally, keeping torso in same plane as legs (avoid rounding chest forward)
options.B: Hips squared forward facing the floor
options.D: Pelvic alignment does not matter in triangle pose

questions.explanation: Keeping hips open laterally maintains alignment in the frontal plane, preventing back strain.

questions.question: Yoga class sequencing 'Cooling down transition' what pose group prepares students for final Savasana?

options.A: Gentle supine twists and forward folds
options.D: Transition poses are not needed before Savasana

questions.explanation: Supine twists and forward bends calm the nervous system, preparing the body for deep rest.

questions.question: Annamaya Kosha physical sheath food?

options.A: The physical body sheath (made of food and structural tissues)
options.D: Koshas do not relate to physical bodies

questions.explanation: Annamaya Kosha is the outer physical sheath, nourished by food and maintained through asana practice.

questions.question: Respect limitation modify poses pain?

options.B: Push clients past pain limits to speed up flexibility gains
options.C: Ignore limitations and teach one advanced sequence to all

questions.explanation: Respecting physical limits prevents injuries and builds a supportive, safe class environment.

questions.question: Parasympathetic activation Chandra Bhedana left?

options.D: Pranayama does not affect nervous systems

questions.explanation: Chandra Bhedana (left nostril) cools the body and activates the parasympathetic response, lowering heart rate.

questions.question: Trikonasana hips open laterally torso?

options.A: Hips open laterally, keeping torso in same plane as legs (avoid rounding chest forward)
options.B: Hips squared forward facing the floor
options.D: Pelvic alignment does not matter in triangle pose

questions.explanation: Keeping hips open laterally maintains alignment in the frontal plane, preventing back strain.

questions.question: Supine twist forward fold Savasana?

options.A: Gentle supine twists and forward folds
options.D: Transition poses are not needed before Savasana

questions.explanation: Supine twists and forward bends calm the nervous system, preparing the body for deep rest.

questions.question: Yoga Teacher – Foundation Level final launch audits why teaching demonstration is key final step?

options.A: Verifies ability to explain alignment cues, demonstrate asana safely, and offer modifications with props

questions.explanation: A teaching demonstration assesses cueing clarity and adjustment safety, verifying readiness to lead classes.

options.C: Building high rise weight training machinery
options.D: Zero spiritual or body alignment check

questions.explanation: Yama life ethics aur external behavior restraint guidelines hai, which forms foundation of yoga path.

options.A: Improving body posture, balance, and establishing correct foot weight distribution alignments
options.C: Increasing heart rate to anaerobic limits fast

questions.explanation: Tadasana all standing asanas ka base posture hai. Ye body awareness, spine stretching aur ground balance improve karta hai.

questions.question: Nadi Shodhana breathing nervous system?

options.A: Calming the nervous system, balancing left/right brain hemispheres and lowering stress levels
options.B: Raising blood pressure rapidly to boost sprint speeds

questions.explanation: Nadi Shodhana breathing loops left (Ida) aur right (Pingala) nadis energy flow ko balance karti hai, relieving mental pressure.

questions.question: Yoga energy locks Bandhas physiology?

options.A: Bandhas (eg Mula Bandha, Uddiyana Bandha, Jalandhara Bandha)
options.D: Locks do not exist in yoga

questions.explanation: Bandhas energy locks hain jo internal prana flow channels validate karte hain during breath retention (Kumbhaka).

questions.question: Paschimottanasana seated forward hamstring stretch?

options.A: Hamstrings and entire posterior spine muscles

questions.explanation: Forward bending hamstrings spine muscles compress stretch improve karta hai, improving pelvic flexibility.

questions.question: Props block straps beginner modifications?

options.A: Supporting students with tight muscles to execute asanas safely without straining alignments
options.C: Using them as heavy lifting weight items
options.D: Props are strictly forbidden in traditional yoga

questions.explanation: Yoga props structure support karte hain for beginners or restricted clients, protecting lumbar region from overload.

questions.question: High blood pressure inversions sirsasana warning?

options.A: Deep inversions (eg Headstand - Sirsasana, Shoulderstand - Sarvangasana)

questions.explanation: Inversions head arteries par blood pressure excess build karte hain, which can trigger vessel damage in hypertension cases.

questions.question: Nasal passages Jala Neti saline water?

options.A: Nasal passages (using lukewarm saline water and neti pot)

questions.explanation: Jala Neti nasal cleaning and sinus disinfection checklist target karta hai, clearing respiratory pathways.
